output_approval.authority_effect: field-level recovery routing and status override only

approved_historical_role.decision: The six printed centimeter labels were intended as approximate production targets. Treating them as exact per-view pixel calibrators was a misunderstanding of the original source role.

core_result.mixed_boundary_result: Step 05 is not invalid as a whole. Pixel convention, panel coordinate spaces, world-axis meanings, view normals, source orientation marks, semantic IDs, and blocked physical correspondence remain valid. Its future units policy and any exact-centimeter interpretation of labeled stations require override.
core_result.first_affected_authorized_rule: steps/STEP_06_CONTRACT.md lines 78-81: calibrate from source-authored dimension endpoints with cm_per_px = declared_real_span_cm / pixel_span
core_result.causal_assumption: The process assumed the printed values were exact real spans suitable for independent panel calibration before proving cross-view scale and projection coherence.

smallest_sufficient_recovery: Approve this reassessment output and field-level override.
Prepare a separate scale-authority recovery contract that chooses final production bounds and creates an original-coordinate global transform manifest.
Create and visibly review derivative normalized panels and distortion records under that later contract.
Rerun only affected centimeter/world and cross-view integration records while preserving raw pixel evidence.
Revise pending Step 10 item I10-001 and every dependent candidate option after transformed-frame approval.
Do not begin Step 11 until the revised Step 10 decision surface is approved.
